response:
a night-shift network engineer monitors an organization's infrastructure, which consists of a series of interconnected servers. the reliability of each server $ s_i $ in the network can be modeled by a continuous random variable $ r_i $ following a normal distribution $ n(\mu_i, \sigma_i^2) $. the engineer also collaborates with the cybersecurity team to mitigate potential threats, which can be modeled as a poisson process with a rate $ \lambda $ of attacks per hour.

the probability that at least 9 out of the 10 servers will have a reliability of at least 0.95 at any given time is calculated using the binomial distribution. the probability that a single server has a reliability of at least 0.95 is $ p = 1 - \frac{1}{\sqrt{2\pi}\sigma_i \sqrt{2\pi}\sigma_i} \exp\left(-\frac{(0.95 - \mu_i)^2}{2\sigma_i^2}\right) $. the probability that at least 9 out of 10 servers will have a reliability of at least 0.95 is then calculated using the binomial probability formula. the probability that exactly 5 attacks will be detected during a 2-hour monitoring period is calculated using the poisson distribution. the probability that exactly 5 attacks will be detected during a 2-hour monitoring period is $ \frac{e^{-\lambda t} (\lambda t)^5}{5!} $, where $ \lambda = 3 $ and $ t = 2 $. is there anything else i can help with?
